reason: Both answers correctly identify alert noise as the root cause, correctly note the 6-person weekly rotation itself is fine, and both explicitly warn against restructuring the schedule before fixing signal. Both propose the same concrete alert-audit process (was it actionable and urgent -> delete/downgrade/tune). A adds routing non-urgent alerts to business hours and a per-shift page budget; B names a recurring weekly page-review ritual, matching the review-every-page-weekly point more explicitly, and frames follow-the-sun as a later consideration rather than an immediate fix. Neither explicitly addresses reduced sprint commitment during on-call. No factual errors in either; both are concise and land on the same correct recommendation, making them comparable overall.
